items.front: Random RNAV nonradar: max distance between points
items.back: 500 miles.

items.front: Random RNAV nonradar: protected airspace either side
items.back: 4 miles either side of route centerline.

items.front: Random RNAV nonradar: altitude requirement
items.back: At or above the highest MIA along the projected route segment including protected airspace.

items.front: Random impromptu route definition
items.back: Direct course initiated by ATC or pilot during flight from present position to a NAVAID, waypoint, fix, or airport.

items.front: Point-to-point route definition
items.back: Route segment that begins and ends with a published NAVAID, waypoint, fix, or airport.

items.front: Substitute ATS route phraseology
items.back: SUBSTITUTE (ATS route) FROM (fix) to (fix).

items.front: Lat/long fix phraseology
items.back: DIRECT (degrees) DEGREES, (minutes) MINUTES (N/S), (degrees) DEGREES, (minutes) MINUTES (E/W).

items.front: ATS route types (4-4-1a)
items.back: VICTOR, J (route), Q (route), Tango (route), IR (route), or SUBSTITUTE route.

items.front: RNAV route transition: radar monitoring requirement
items.back: Required, except for point-to-point routes via an impromptu route.

items.front: Route structure transition methods (4-4-2)
items.back: Vector, SID/STAR, climb/descend via radials, direct to/between NAVAIDs, climb/descend via ATS route, climb/descend on specified radials, RNAV random route.

items.front: Degree-distance fix components (4-4-3)
items.back: 1) Location identifier  2) Azimuth in degrees magnetic  3) Distance in miles from NAVAID. Example: 'MKE 030025.'

items.front: Military degree-distance: max distance between fixes below FL 180
items.back: 80 miles.

items.front: Military degree-distance: max distance between fixes FL 180 and above
items.back: 260 miles.

items.front: Military degree-distance: max distance for celestial navigation
items.back: 260 miles at all altitudes.

items.front: Military degree-distance fixes: reporting requirement
items.back: Compulsory reporting points, but ATC may authorize omission when traffic conditions permit.

items.front: Military degree-distance: flight plan filing deadline
items.back: At least 2 hours before estimated time of departure.

items.front: Military degree-distance: traffic flow conformity radius
items.back: Within 75 miles of departure and arrival airports unless prior coordination effected.

items.front: Alternative routes: which aircraft affected
items.back: Aircraft that are NOT RNAV capable.

items.front: Alternative route word when using charted routes
items.back: 'Substitute' immediately precedes the alternative route.

items.front: Alternative route methods (4-4-4)
items.back: 1) Charted route with 'substitute.' 2) NAVAID radials/courses/azimuths. 3) Direct to/between NAVAIDs. 4) Vectors.

items.front: Action for RNAV aircraft during NAVAID outage
items.back: Inform them that they will proceed to the NAVAID location of the outage.

items.front: Class G airspace route inclusion rule
items.back: Include routes through Class G only when requested by the pilot.

items.front: Separation in Class G airspace
items.back: Not applicable. Traffic advisories and safety alerts apply to aircraft in direct communication with ATC.

items.front: Random RNAV or MTR through Class G: pilot intent
items.back: Considered a request by the pilot to include Class G airspace in the clearance.

items.front: When NOT to issue direct routing off flight plan route
items.back: 1) Aircraft part of known TM initiative. 2) Route within protected segment (coordinate with TMU/ATCSCC if amendment needed).

items.front: En route: routing past last fix in your airspace
items.back: Do not issue unless requested by pilot or operational necessity dictates.

items.front: Protected segment amendment: ARTCC TMU coordination
items.back: Outside their facility: with ATCSCC. Terminal TMU: with overlying ARTCC TMU. ATCS: with TMU.

items.front: 4-4-6 exception for LOAs/SOPs
items.back: Does NOT preclude routing clearances conforming to LOAs/SOPs, required for separation, or complying with TM flow initiatives.
